nodes.label: Parallel Agent Execution via asyncio.gather
nodes.rationale: All attack types now run concurrently via asyncio.gather with per-agent timeout enforcement (default 30s, configurable). Improves throughput and ensures fairness across attack species.

nodes.label: API Key Authentication for Web Dashboard
nodes.rationale: CRUCIBLE_API_KEY env var gates the web dashboard. Dashboard warns explicitly when not set.
nodes.norm_label: api key authentication for web dashboard

nodes.label: Attack Extension Protocol (Adding New Attack Types)
nodes.source_location: Adding a new attack type section
nodes.rationale: New attack types require: class in attacks/strategies.py inheriting BaseAdversarialAgent, implement generate_mutations() and apply_mutation(), register in ATTACK_REGISTRY in runner.py, add tests in tests/test_crucible.py, update CHANGELOG.
nodes.norm_label: attack extension protocol (adding new attack types)

nodes.label: Evolutionary Pressure as Intelligence
nodes.rationale: Crucible is not a testing framework — it applies evolutionary pressure to CI/CD infrastructure. Agents are purely algorithmic (no LLM, no external API, no cloud). The evolutionary selection mechanism (fitness, extinction, shadow promotion) is the intelligence, not an external model.

nodes.label: Resilience Score (0–100, Grades A–F)
nodes.rationale: Four weighted components: survival rate (40%), blast containment (25%), recovery speed (20%), coverage breadth (15%). Scores stale after 30 days. Grades A>=90, B>=75, C>=60, D>=40, F<40.

nodes.label: Agent Fitness Scoring Mechanism
nodes.rationale: Fitness 0-100. Agents that trigger failures gain fitness; those finding nothing lose it. Below fitness 20 after 5+ attempts -> extinct, logged to failure cemetery.

nodes.label: Shadow Agents (--shadow flag)
nodes.rationale: Every production agent spawns a shadow running alternative mutations on a copy of the target. Shadow never touches the real target. If shadow trigger rate exceeds production by 20% for 3+ consecutive runs, shadow is promoted and the event is logged.

nodes.label: Replayable Traces (.crucible JSON format)
nodes.rationale: Every run writes a .crucible JSON file to traces/. Core data artifact — scoring, patterns, evolution are all derived from traces. Traces are reproducible and shareable for postmortems.

nodes.label: runner.py — Single Orchestration Layer
nodes.rationale: runner.py is the only file that knows about all layers. Engine, agents, scorer, and memory do not import each other — only runner.py orchestrates them. This is an explicit architectural constraint.

nodes.label: Demo Mode (create_demo_target)
nodes.rationale: Demo mode always works without any real pipeline. create_demo_target() produces a realistic synthetic target. Tests use this to enable CI without real workflow files.

nodes.label: Darwin Runtime (v1.0 roadmap target)
nodes.rationale: v1.0 goal: self-evolving attack strategies, full evolutionary agent infrastructure. Extends current fitness/extinction mechanics to autonomous strategy generation.
